title: Minuria tridens
taxonomy: Minuria tridens is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, and it belongs to the genus Minuria. It is native to Australia and is commonly found in the eastern and southern regions of the country. The species is a type of annual or perennial herb, and it produces small white or yellow flowers. The classification of Minuria tridens places it within the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. Further classification includes the subclass Rosidae and the clade core eudicots.

intro: Minuria tridens, also known as the three-toothed minuria, is a flowering plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Minuria tridens include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Minuria tridens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the most common preparations including a decoction of the leaves and flowers for topical application or oral consumption.
